title: chrysopogon elongatus
intro: Chrysopogon elongatus, also known as Vettivera, is a type of grass native to India and Southeast Asia, and is a member of the Poaceae family. The plant's roots have been used in traditional medicine for centuries due to their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Chrysopogon elongatus include reducing stress and anxiety, improving sleep quality, and alleviating symptoms of arthritis and other inflammatory conditions. Chrysopogon elongatus is often used in herbal preparations such as essential oils, teas, and infusions, which can be applied topically or ingested to reap its medicinal benefits.
